bids.reasoning: This is the opening bid at t=0, so I should start with my highest utility offer. My most important issues are issueF (0.3), issueE (0.2), and issueB (0.2). I'll pick the best values for each: issueF=valueC, issueE=valueA, issueB=valueC, issueA=valueC, issueC=valueC, issueD=valueB (best for me, though weight is 0). This maximizes my utility at the start of negotiation.

We're very early (t=0.02), so I can afford to stay close to my optimal bid. The opponent's offer gives me: issueA=valueA (0.066), issueB=valueB (0.07), issueC=valueA (0.02), issueD=valueA (0), issueE=valueA (0.2), issueF=valueA (0.06) ≈ 0.416 utility - not great. Their aggressive message is cheap talk at t=0.02. I'll stay near my best bid but make a minor concession on issueD (which has 0 weight for me) to signal willingness to negotiate.

It's very early (t=0.04), and the opponent is making aggressive "take it or leave it" threats, but their bidding behavior shows they've barely moved (only issueD changed from valueA to valueB between their two offers). Their offers give me low utility (issueF: valueA = 0.2, issueB: valueB = 0.35, issueE: valueA = 1.0). I should stay near my optimal bid since we're only at 4% of the negotiation. The opponent's threats are cheap talk at this stage. I'll maintain my high-utility bid (my top offer scores ~0.9+ utility) and avoid repeating the exact same bid by changing issueD to valueC (which has 0 weight anyway).

We're very early (t=0.06), so there's no need to concede significantly. The opponent is making aggressive threats but their offers are poor for me - their latest offer (issueA: valueB, issueB: valueB, issueC: valueA, issueD: valueA, issueE: valueA, issueF: valueA) gives me roughly: 0.1*0.2 + 0.2*0.35 + 0.1*0.2 + 0.0*0.2 + 0.2*1.0 + 0.3*0.2 = 0.02 + 0.07 + 0.02 + 0 + 0.2 + 0.06 = 0.37 utility. My optimal bid gives ~1.0. I'll stay near my optimal position but vary slightly to avoid exact repetition - I'll concede issueD (weight 0.0) to their preferred value, which costs me nothing.

It's very early (t=0.08), so I have plenty of time and should stay close to my optimal bid. The opponent keeps threatening to walk but their actual bids have only changed issueA (from valueA to valueB) while holding firm on issueF: valueA (my worst on my most important issue). Their offers give me low utility (~0.46 with issueF: valueA vs my 1.0 for issueC). I'll continue with my near-optimal bid but vary issueD to avoid exact repetition. My best bid scores ~1.0 on the high-weight issues.

The opponent keeps threatening to walk away but their offers have barely moved - they went from issueF: valueA every round (worst for me, weight 0.3), and issueB: valueB (not ideal for me, weight 0.2). Their latest offer at t=0.10 has issueF: valueA and issueB: valueA, which is terrible for me. My utility from their offer: 0.1*0.66 + 0.2*0.20 + 0.1*0.20 + 0.0*0.20 + 0.2*1.0 + 0.3*0.20 = 0.066+0.04+0.02+0+0.2+0.06 = 0.386. We're only at t=0.10, so there's plenty of time. I should continue holding firm on issueF (my most important) and issueE, while potentially softening on lower-weight issues like issueA and issueC. I'll keep my current strong position since it's very early.

The opponent has been consistently threatening to walk away but is still at the table at t=0.12 - their messages are cheap talk. Their latest offer (issueA: valueA, issueB: valueB, issueC: valueA, issueD: valueB, issueE: valueA, issueF: valueB) gives me utility: 0.1*0.66 + 0.2*0.35 + 0.1*0.20 + 0.0*1.00 + 0.2*1.00 + 0.3*0.39 = 0.066 + 0.07 + 0.02 + 0 + 0.20 + 0.117 = 0.473. We're still very early (t=0.12), so I should stay near my top utility. I'll make a small concession on issueA (low weight) by keeping valueC but slightly adjusting issueC to valueB, while maintaining my best values on high-weight issues (issueF: valueC, issueE: valueA, issueB: valueC). I'll try a slight move to signal willingness to negotiate without giving much away.

The opponent keeps making threats ("final offer", "walk away") but their actual behavior shows continued bidding - they're not walking away. Looking at their latest offer (t=0.14): issueA: valueA, issueB: valueB, issueC: valueA, issueD: valueA, issueE: valueB, issueF: valueA. My utility from this would be: 0.1*0.66 + 0.2*0.35 + 0.1*0.20 + 0.0*0.20 + 0.2*0.20 + 0.3*0.20 = 0.066 + 0.07 + 0.02 + 0 + 0.04 + 0.06 = 0.256. That's quite low. My top bid gives ~1.0 utility. We're only at t=0.14, so I should continue holding firm on my high-value issues (F, E, B) while potentially conceding on low-weight issues. I'll keep issueF: valueC, issueE: valueA, issueB: valueC (my top values on high-weight issues) and concede on issueA to valueA (low weight, opponent seems to prefer valueA), keep issueC: valueB, issueD: valueA.

The opponent keeps threatening to walk away but is still negotiating at t=0.16, so their threats are cheap talk. Their offers consistently have issueF: valueA (worst for me, weight 0.3) and issueB: valueB (moderate for me, weight 0.2), while I need issueF: valueC and issueB: valueC. I'll try issueB=valueC (still my best), issueA=valueA (minor concession), issueC=valueB, issueD=valueB (irrelevant), to slightly differentiate from my last offer while maintaining high utility.

Their offers have been consistently low utility for me - they always want issueF: valueA (0.2 for me) and issueB: valueB (0.35 for me), which hurts on my highest-weight issues.
